title: Johnson & Johnson — Lung — Synthesis
html:
Lung Cancer Cross-Study Synthesis

Generated: 2026-04-24 Studies analyzed: 4 Files parsed: Rybrevant PET Q4'25, Lung OCE Q4'25, Social Listening Full 2025, Lung OCE Q1'26 Findings extracted: Manual extraction from ~195K chars of parsed PPTX content Method: Cross-study pattern matching against Vinoth's thesis tracker

1. Cross-Study Patterns

Pattern 1: Presence Is Strong, Conversion Is Broken — Confirmed Across All Studies

This is the single most consistent finding across every ZoomRx study in the lung portfolio.

PET Q4'25: J&J reps lead SOV at 54% vs AZ 46%. Call frequency increasing. But branded closing lags TAG+Chemo (46% vs 55%). J&J has "achieved peak call quality" yet AZ retains a narrow lead in getting HCPs to actually commit.

OCE Q4'25: J&J and AZ OCEs are exactly tied at 50% SOV. J&J leads on message recall (47% vs 37%) and call quality (82% vs 72%). Yet APPs are STILL more likely to advocate TAG vs RYB+LAZ — "primarily due to perceived safety concerns and prophylaxis-related treatment complexity."

Social Listening: Only 20% of Reddit EGFR NSCLC conversations mention Rybrevant. Of those, only 40% are actual patient/caregiver experiences vs 75% for Tagrisso. Rybrevant's online presence is news-driven (OS data, trial results), not experience-driven.

What this means together: J&J is winning the field activity war but losing the behavior change war. Reps are present, OCEs are effective, messages are recalled — but the physician/APP doesn't change what they prescribe. The barrier is downstream of awareness. It's either comfort (safety), inertia (familiarity), or both.

Pattern 2: Safety Perception Is the Primary Conversion Blocker

PET Q4'25: Reps push OS and CNS messages toward the end of calls along with safety messages. NCCN guideline message is most recalled and impactful, but CNS/OS recall has "dropped substantially during community HCP interactions." Safety is part of the closing package, not the opening argument.

OCE Q4'25: "VTEs trigger higher discontinuation intent relative to other AEs." COCOON derm results message has low recall but high impact when recalled — meaning OCEs aren't delivering it enough. APPs cite "safety concerns and prophylaxis-related treatment complexity" as the primary reason they advocate TAG over RYB+LAZ.

Social Listening: RYBREVANT has 3x more safety/AE management discussion than Tagrisso on Facebook (28% vs 8%). Patients and caregivers raise concerns about dermatologic toxicities, IRRs, and edema. "Side-effect management is largely reactive; uncontrolled AEs frequently disrupt treatment continuity."

What this means together: Safety isn't just a messaging problem — it's the structural barrier. Patients experience AEs, APPs see the complexity, physicians hear about it, and the prescribing default stays TAGRISSO. The COCOON data should be mitigating this, but OCEs aren't delivering the derm management message enough.

Pattern 3: The Academic-Community Divide Is Widening

PET Q4'25: J&J rep SOV improvement is "primarily driven by increased call frequency among academic HCPs." Meanwhile, community HCPs show declining recall of OS and CNS messages. Visual aid usage is increasing in academic interactions but dropping in community. Rep-led narrative impact is "stronger in academic interactions in Q4'25 while it drops within community interactions."

OCE Q4'25: J&J OCEs engage APPs with "lower baseline comfort managing RYB+LAZ patients" and successfully elevate comfort — but this is concentrated in the interactions that happen, not a market-wide effect.

Social Listening: Patient and caregiver conversations show uncertainty about next steps after Tagrisso progression, and misconceptions about RYBREVANT being "only for exon-20." Community-level awareness of RYBREVANT as a 1L option is limited.

What this means together: Academic adoption is happening — the data shows it in the PET metrics. But community oncologists are being left behind. J&J's field force is doubling down on academic where they're already winning, instead of investing in the harder community conversion. This is rational short-term but strategically dangerous — community is where the volume lives.

Pattern 4: FASPRO Is a Measurement Gap — No Study Has Captured Its Impact Yet

PET Q4'25: Fielding period Oct-Dec 2025. FASPRO approved late Q4. No meaningful FASPRO signal in this data.

OCE Q4'25: Same fielding period. No FASPRO impact.

Social Listening: Study period Nov 2024 - Oct 2025. "RYBREVANT FASPRO approval falls outside the time period considered for this study."

What this means together: Q1 2026 earnings show $257M revenue with 18.8% sequential growth attributed to FASPRO. But NONE of ZoomRx's tracking studies have measured the FASPRO effect yet. The Q1 2026 waves are the first opportunity to see whether SubQ is changing prescriber behavior. This is a critical data gap — and an opportunity for ZoomRx to be the first to quantify the FASPRO effect.

Pattern 5: Tagrisso's Real Moat Is Patient/Caregiver Experience, Not Clinical Data

Social Listening: 80% of Reddit EGFR NSCLC conversations are about Tagrisso. 75% of Tagrisso mentions are patient/caregiver experiences. By contrast, only 40% of Rybrevant mentions are patient experiences — the rest are news articles.

PET Q4'25: Despite J&J leading SOV, Tagrisso "conversations" at the HCP level are anchored in years of prescribing experience, not promotional activity.

What this means together: Tagrisso's moat isn't just physician familiarity — it extends to the patient community. Patients research online, find Tagrisso-dominant conversations, and arrive at oncologist visits with a default expectation. This creates a reinforcing loop that promotional activity alone can't break. J&J needs a patient-level strategy (which FASPRO helps — convenience story) alongside the HCP-level one.

2. Thesis Check

Thesis 1: MARIPOSA OS determines Rybrevant's ceiling

Status: STRENGTHENED MARIPOSA OS data is published (25% risk reduction, median OS not reached vs 36.7 months for Tagrisso). FLAURA-2 OS also read out (47.5 months with osi+chemo vs 37.6 months). Both show OS benefit for combination approaches. The PET data shows NCCN guideline message (which incorporates MARIPOSA) is the most impactful message. OS IS moving the conversation — but slowly. Action: The ceiling question is shifting from "will OS help?" to "how fast does OS translate to share?" Track NCCN message impact longitudinally.

Thesis 2: Physician inertia > efficacy

Status: STRONGLY CONFIRMED This is the most validated thesis across all studies. J&J leads SOV, delivers effective messages, has published OS data — and TAG still leads in branded closing, advocacy, and patient-level conversation. Inertia is the dominant force. However, Q1 2026 earnings (18.8% sequential growth) suggest FASPRO may be the first tool that cracks inertia by addressing the convenience dimension rather than the data dimension. Action: Monitor Q1 2026 PET/SFEA data for FASPRO-driven behavior change. If inertia breaks, it breaks through convenience, not efficacy.

Thesis 3: Presence without depth is fixable

Status: STRENGTHENED WITH NUANCE The PET data confirms: reps are present but mid-story is fragmented. NCCN leads, but OS and CNS are pushed late. "~40% of interactions do not lead with 1L setting discussions." OCE data adds: COCOON derm message has low recall but highest impact when recalled — meaning the depth is available but not being delivered. This IS fixable: better call flow, earlier OS/CNS messaging, more consistent COCOON delivery. Action: Recommend to J&J: restructure call flow to front-load OS after NCCN, and mandate COCOON derm delivery in every OCE interaction.

Thesis 4: ADCs will own post-TKI space

Status: UNCHANGED — INSUFFICIENT INTERNAL DATA No ZoomRx study currently tracks ADC awareness or preference. AstraZeneca's earnings confirm TROPION-Lung15 and TROPION-Lung14 are in Phase 3. Merck/Daiichi withdrew patritumab deruxtecan (noted in social listening timeline). The competitive landscape is shifting but ZoomRx has no measurement. Action: Propose ADC tracking module to J&J — even a 5-question addition to PET/SFEA would create visibility no competitor has.

Thesis 5: Community is the volume game, Rybrevant isn't built for it yet

Status: STRONGLY CONFIRMED, BUT EVOLVING PET data shows the academic-community gap widening: message recall dropping in community, visual aid usage declining, narrative impact falling. Social listening shows limited community-level patient awareness. However, FASPRO changes the equation — Q1 2026 revenue growth suggests community prescribers are starting to move. Action: Next PET/SFEA wave MUST segment community vs academic adoption of FASPRO. If FASPRO drives community adoption, this thesis gets retired or significantly revised.

3. Contradictions & Surprises

  1. J&J OCE interactions are rated MORE impactful than AZ in Q4 2025, yet advocacy still favors TAG. If interactions are better, why isn't behavior changing? This suggests impact metrics (call quality, comfort) are not translating to prescribing intent. The measurement may be capturing "good conversation" not "intention to change."

  2. Message recall is improving for J&J, but branded closing is not. PET shows J&J leading message recall and effectiveness, yet AZ leads branded closing 55% vs 46%. The gap between recall and action is growing, not shrinking. Messages are landing — they're just not converting.

  3. COCOON derm message: lowest recall, highest impact. This is the single most actionable finding. The message that most reduces discontinuation intent is the one least likely to be delivered. This isn't a content problem — it's a delivery prioritization problem.

  4. Social listening shows Rybrevant sentiment is slightly MORE positive than Tagrisso (17% positive vs 12% for Tagrisso on Reddit). Yet Tagrisso dominates conversation volume 80:20. The minority that discusses Rybrevant is more positive — but they're a small minority.

4. Gaps — What's Missing

  1. No FASPRO impact data — The single biggest market event (SubQ approval) is not captured in any ZoomRx study fielded to date. Q1 2026 waves are critical.

  2. No ADC tracking — AstraZeneca has 3 ADCs in Phase 3 and Merck withdrew one. ZoomRx has zero visibility into ADC awareness or preference among EGFR NSCLC physicians.

  3. No community adoption segmentation — PET tracks academic vs community indirectly, but there's no dedicated module measuring FASPRO adoption by practice setting.

  4. No patient-level data integration — Social listening shows the patient experience gap, but this isn't connected to the PET/OCE data. Understanding how patient expectations influence physician behavior is the missing link.

  5. No competitive response tracking — AstraZeneca's FLAURA2 strategy (osi+chemo) is a direct response to MARIPOSA, but ZoomRx has limited visibility into how AZ is messaging FLAURA2 vs Tagrisso monotherapy.

5. Priority Actions for Vinoth

  1. Push COCOON derm delivery as an immediate fix. Tell J&J: "Your most impactful message has your lowest recall. If every OCE interaction included COCOON derm results, discontinuation intent would drop. This is the lowest-hanging fruit in the portfolio." This is a concrete, data-backed recommendation from cross-study evidence.

  2. Design a FASPRO adoption tracker for Q1 2026 wave. The earnings data shows FASPRO is driving growth, but ZoomRx can't tell J&J WHERE it's working (academic vs community), WHY it's working (convenience vs efficacy reframe), or HOW FAST it's working (new prescriber acquisition vs existing prescriber deepening). Propose this as an overlay on existing PET infrastructure.

  3. Propose cross-TA "presence vs depth" analysis to J&J. This pattern (SOV leadership without conversion) repeats in Lung PET, Lung OCE, and likely in GU MSL and IMM FRM. A cross-TA presentation showing the same pattern and proposing a unified measurement/intervention framework is a new engagement stream.

  4. Add ADC awareness questions to the next PET/SFEA wave. Even 3-5 questions tracking HERTHENA-Lung02 and Dato-DXd awareness would make ZoomRx the first to quantify the ADC threat in EGFR NSCLC. Propose this before J&J asks for it.

  5. Brief the team on the academic-community divergence. The Q4 PET data shows community performance declining while academic improves. If this continues, J&J's field ROI is concentrating in a shrinking prescriber pool. This is the kind of insight that earns a QBR slot.

Cross-study synthesis generated from 4 ZoomRx deliverables (PET Q4'25, OCE Q4'25, Social Listening 2025, OCE data). Thesis-driven: findings evaluated against Vinoth's standing POV on the EGFR NSCLC market.

Back to TA Knowledge Hub | Home
